results.title: Staff Product Manager – AI – 100% Remote – EMEA - Inclusivelyremote
highlights:
Staff Product Manager – AI – 100% Remote – EMEA - Inclusivelyremote
[...]
# Staff Product Manager – AI – 100% Remote – EMEA
[...]
NOTE: This is a FULLY remote role, but the candidate must be within EMEA to collaborate with their team, peers, and internal customers. You do not have to be in the specific country or city shown in this listing, but please only apply if you are physically based within EMEA.
[...]
Trusted by 20,000+ property managers worldwide, Hostaway is an industry leading, AI-powered vacation rental management platform designed for professional short-term rental operators. Hostaway brings everything together for greater efficiency and faster growth.
[...]
Hostaway AI creates value by streamlining routine work, improving decision-making, and simplifying complex tasks across the property management lifecycle. As Staff Product Manager, AI, you will define and drive Hostaway’s company-wide AI product strategy, identifying where AI can create meaningful customer and business value across our platform.
[...]
This role is for an AI-native product leader who combines strong product judgment, customer empathy, and analytical rigor. You do not need to build AI models yourself, but you must understand how LLM-based products work in practice, how to evaluate them with discipline, and how to translate a rapidly evolving AI landscape into clear roadmap decisions and reliable product experiences.
[...]
### What you will do:
[...]
- Define and own Hostaway’s AI product strategy and roadmap across the company.
[...]
- Plan and execute thorough customer research and analysis to deeply understand the needs and pain of property managers translating those insights along with legal requirements into product specifications and user stories.
[...]
- Identify the highest-impact AI opportunities across customer workflows, product areas, and internal operations.
[...]
- Partner with engineering, design, data, and GTM teams to deliver AI-powered experiences that solve real customer problems.
[...]
- Establish clear approaches to AI evaluation, including quality benchmarks, eval sets, failure modes, and success metrics.
[...]
- Use product, customer, and performance data to prioritize investments and improve AI quality over time.
[...]
- Assess build vs. buy decisions across LLMs, APIs, AI tools, and third-party solutions.
[...]
- Stay on top of changes in models, tooling, and AI product patterns, and translate them into product strategy.
[...]
- Influence senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams to align around clear priorities, tradeoffs, and outcomes.
[...]
## Job requirements
[...]
- 7+ years of product management experience, ideally in SaaS, including ownership of complex and ambiguous product areas.
[...]
- Strong practical understanding of LLMs and AI product development, including prompting, model tradeoffs, latency, cost, context limits, and quality risks.
[...]
- Experience defining and using evaluation sets, quality benchmarks, and feedback loops for AI-powered products.
[...]
- Experience with agentic AI concepts, including tool use, planning, and orchestration.
[...]
- Experience evaluating and integrating off-the-shelf AI solutions, including APIs, SaaS tools, and third-party platforms.
[...]
- Highly data-driven, with strong judgment in using metrics, experiments, and customer insight to make decisions.
[...]
- Demonstrated AI-native curiosity and fluency, with a clear habit of staying current as models, tools, and best practices evolve.
[...]
### What success looks like:
[...]
- AI-powered features are seamlessly embedded into Hostaway’s SaaS product.
- Customer workflows feel smarter, faster, and more personalized thanks to the integrations you build with the team.
- AI services are stable, observable, and easy to maintain.
- You become the go-to person for the Hostaway AI product vision and capabilities across the company.

results.title: SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (REMOTE OPPORTUNITY) | Wfh
highlights:
SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (REMOTE OPPORTUNITY) | Wfh
[...]
# SENIOR DATA SCIENTIST (REMOTE OPPORTUNITY)
[...]
Hyatt Hotels Corporation seeks an enthusiastic Senior Data Scientist to join our Data Science/Decision Science team. In this role, you will translate business objectives into advanced AI, Machine Learning and Operations Research models, help build data-driven decision-making solutions across key areas of the business, directly contributing to Hyatt’s mission of delivering distinctive experience for our guests.
[...]
You will have the opportunity to tackle complex business challenges by applying cutting-edge machine learning and operations research techniques, with a focus on building optimization models and decision-support assets. As part of a growing, collaborative, and highly visible Data Science team, you will work alongside talented professionals in data analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI, all passionate about transforming the future of hospitality through the power of data science.
[...]
- The Role
- Translate business problems in a variety of business areas into well-defined data science projects, ensuring alignment with business goals, scope, and defined KPIs.
[...]
- Design, implement, and optimize advanced machine learning and optimization models to address complex business challenges.
[...]
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including engineering, data, and business stakeholders, ensuring clear communication, seamless integration of data-driven solutions.
[...]
- Monitor model performance in production, refining algorithms and processes to adapt to real-world data and evolving business needs.
[...]
- Create and maintain detailed documentation for models, methodologies, and workflows to support team knowledge-sharing.
[...]
- Conduct testing and validation of models to ensure robustness, scalability, and reliability in production environments.
[...]
- Present data-driven insights, findings, and product outcomes to stakeholders in a clear, actionable manner.
[...]
- Stay updated on the latest advancements in machine learning and optimization, integrating innovative techniques and tools into projects.
[...]
- Experience Required:
[...]
- Master’s degree in computer science, statistics, industrial engineering, or related fields required, PhD preferred.
- 5+ years of experience in data science, operations research, or related area (2+ years for candidates with PhD).
- Expertise in operations research modeling (LP, IP, MIP) and tools (CPLEX, Gurobi, etc).
[...]
- Expertise in building machine learning models, including supervised, unsupervised, and deep learning methods.
[...]
- Expertise in feature engineering, model evaluation, and hyperparameter tuning.
[...]
- Expertise in Python, SQL, and Spark, and a broad array of machine learning frameworks (Scikit-Learn, XGBoost, Tensorflow, PyTorch, MXNet, LLM, etc).
[...]
- Experience in developing and deploying solutions in a Cloud environment (AWS, Azure, GCP) with large datasets.
[...]
- Experience with streaming data architectures.

results.title: Senior ML Engineer (Remote opportunity) | Hirevector
highlights:
Senior ML Engineer (Remote opportunity) | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# Senior ML Engineer (Remote opportunity)

Posted May 30, 2026

- Hyatt Hotels Corporation seeks an enthusiastic Senior ML Engineer to join our Data Science and Machine Learning department. In this role, you will be collaborating closely with the broader Data and Analytics team, where you’ll be instrumental in continuing to make Hyatt a leading hospitality company.
- Requirements
- Design and implement end-to-end ML systems, including data ingestion, feature processing, model training, and model serving
- Architect and deploy scalable AI services supporting real-time and batch inference use cases
- Build and maintain ML infrastructure across cloud environments (e.g., EC2, EKS, SageMaker, specialized inference hardware)
- Develop and evolve MLOps platforms, including training pipelines, deployment workflows, feature stores, and model observability
- Implement CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code patterns to automate model lifecycle management
- Optimize model training and inference performance for cost, latency, and hardware efficiency
- Monitor production ML systems for accuracy, reliability, and operational health
- Partner cross-functionally with data engineering, architecture, governance, and security teams to ensure compliant and scalable solutions
- Mentor team members on ML engineering, system design, and operational best practices
- Contribute to special initiatives that advance AI platform maturity and engineering standards
- Benefits
- Annual allotment of free hotel stays at Hyatt hotels globally
- Flexible work schedule
- Work-life benefits including wellbeing initiatives such as a complimentary Headspace subscription, and a discount at the on-site fitness center
- A global family assistance policy with paid time off following the birth or adoption of a child as well as financial assistance for adoption
- Paid Time Off, Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K with company match

results.title: Lead Cloud AI Platforms Engineer | Hirequorum
highlights:
Lead Cloud AI Platforms Engineer | Hirequorum
[...]
# Lead Cloud AI Platforms Engineer
[...]
This is your chance to be a part of an in-house Technology team that's creating consumer-facing, cutting-edge technologies revolutionizing the hospitality industry around the world! As a Lead Cloud AI Platforms Engineer, you will bring your technical skills to a hospitality company with an award-winning culture. On the Cloud Engineering team reporting to the Senior Manager Cloud Platforms, you will support projects including projects for Hilton's Data Science, Distribution, and Call Center teams.
[...]
- Be the lead technical expert on a team of Cloud AI Platform Engineers.
- Communicate with team members and keep project delivery on target.
- Identify opportunities for AI, ML and data infrastructure automation using Infrastructure as Code, improvements in configuration management, monitoring, alerting, and create solutions for complex cloud infrastructure challenges.
[...]
- Support Hilton's Cloud AI and ML platforms including LLM application development, prompt engineering, data engineering, LLM safeguards, testing, and go-to-market strategies.
[...]
- Support the Hilton Hybrid Cloud environment, including
[...]
support for operations and support teams, participate in on-call rotations, and work alongside other engineering teams to ensure adherence to Hilton standards and processes.
[...]
- Be a technical expert to different parts of the business, consult business and application teams on AI and ML cloud patterns, and build AI and ML solutions using infrastructure as code tools (IAC) and processes and other popular cloud tools.
- Assist teams to deploy workloads into AWS Bedrock, AWS SageMaker Studio, OpenSearch, and other cloud AI and ML technologies.
[...]
- You Have These Minimum Qualifications
- Five (5) years of professional work experience in Technology or related field
- Two (2) years of professional work experience in Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, or Data Science
- Experience with AWS cloud architecture, especially its core services (e.g., EC2, S3, EKS, RDS, networking) and an understanding of its AI, ML and data offerings.
[...]
- Knowledge of AWS Bedrock, SageMaker AI, LLMs, MCP, agentic frameworks, and other cloud machine learning technologies, deployment patterns and wide variety of AWS components.
[...]
- Experience with containerization and Kubernetes and automation technologies and processes using CI/CD tools.
- Experience in cloud platform engineering and tooling, including Terraform, AWS Command Line, Linux shell scripting, or Python including common AI and ML libraries
[...]
- It Would
[...]
You Have
- Bachelor's Degree, or Associate's Degree plus 6+ years of Technology related experience, or High School Degree/GED. plus 12+ years of Technology related experience.
- Seven (7) + years of professional work experience in Data Science, Technology or a related field.
- FinOps certifications (Practitioner, Engineer, or Professional)
- Familiarity with AI and ML technologies like LLM app development, prompt engineering, data engineering, AI and ML security, logging and observability.
[...]
Hilton offers its eligible team members a comprehensive benefits package to support their mental and physical well-being, so they can Thrive personally and professionally. The pay range for this role is $110,000 – $175,000 and is determined based on applicable and specialized experience and location. Subject to plan terms and conditions, you will be eligible to participate in the Hilton Annual Incentive (Bonus) Plan, consistent with other team members at the same level and/or position within the Company.

Mandarin Oriental is the award‑winning owner and operator of some of the world’s most luxurious hotels, resorts and residences. Increasingly recognised for creating some of the world’s most sought‑after properties, the Group provides legendary service inspired by Asian heritage while representing the cutting edge of luxury experiences. Mandarin Oriental’s Corporate Office in Hong Kong supports our global portfolio through innovation, digital transformation, and advanced analytics to elevate both guest experience and operational excellence. *About the Job** Based in the Corporate Office in Hong Kong, the AI Solutions Lead will drive the design, development, and deployment of AI capabilities across the Group. The role identifies opportunities where AI can enhance guest experience, optimise operations, and support revenue growth — all while maintaining Mandarin Oriental’s hallmark of personalised, high‑touch service. The position reports to the VP of Data Analytics & AI. *As AI Solutions Lead, you will be responsible for the following:*Strategic LeadershipExecute the Group’s AI strategy in alignment with the results.title: Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host | Wfh
[...]
# Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host
[...]
Guest Experience is responsible for Search and Discovery, Merchandising, and end-to-end online software experience worldwide from top-of-funnel discovery through post-booking engagement. We are looking for a talented Data Scientist to join the Guest Data Science team. This tech lead will be at the core of using AI and casual inference to understand user behavior in our app, detect underlying intent, and to build data products needed to provide a compelling and personalized experience of our app. We build products that help guests find the inventory most relevant to them in our marketplace.
[...]
As a Senior Staff Data Scientist, you will lead innovation efforts to build methodologies to identify and evaluate the preferences important to every guest, at every step of their online discovery funnel. You will be instrumental in shaping our data strategy to drive guest conversion and regular engagement with the Airbnb platform across our Homes, Experiences and Services business verticals.
[...]
The ideal candidate will demonstrate to be a domain expert with granular user behavior and sequence datasets in a marketplace setting. You will hold a high bar for our customer experience and balance a passion for AI with a strong desire to accurately understand user behavior and business success. Your efforts towards personalized content discovery will help make Airbnb feel tailored for you, maintaining a dialogue with our guests to deeply understand their preferences and reflect that understanding back to them, enabling every guest to easily discover the travel they will love.
[...]
- Learn: Develop deep understanding of how guests navigate and re-engage with our app via analysis, research, and by leveraging granular user action and sequence datasets.
[...]
- Partner: With product and engineering, drive technical frameworks and science leadership to explore innovative paradigms for detecting revealed preferences and quantifying online frictions.
[...]
- Build: Write code for prototypes to detect and quantify taxonomy of guest preferences via iterative development of data frameworks, models and artefacts derived from AI toolkits
[...]
- Evaluate: Assess assumptions and efficacy of derived guest preferences via measurement and validating hypotheses linked to online guest action and engagement. Setup experiments and data feedback loops to own a high bar over continuous impact.
[...]
- Your Expertise:
[...]
- Causal inference expertise with marketplace experience
- Preferred domain experience in search, UX discovery, personalized evidence systems
- Advanced degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics or related field.
- 9+ years of industry experience with a PhD (or 12+ years with a Masters)
- Strong in communication with XFN partners in product, engineering, and design to enable data-driven product development with a focus on the user experience.
[...]
- Expert in at least one programming language for data analysis (Python or R) with familiarity in SQL. Comfort with developing proof-of-concept prototypes. Passionate about AI and possessing a learner’s mindset towards LLMs and dynamic systems.

results.title: Head of AI Enablement - hireneXus
highlights:
## Head of AI Enablement
[...]
OF AI & ENABLEMENT
[...]
The company is a heavy Claude user. They use a significant amount of off-the-shelf SaaS, are actively involved in M&A integration, and have begun building internal data and AI tools — but there's no dedicated owner for AI enablement, reusable prompt libraries, capability gap identification, or field-level technology adoption. That gap is this job.
[...]
The Head of AI & Enablement will own the full AI lifecycle — strategy, tool evaluation, pilots, rollout, and measurement — across the entire brand portfolio. In year one, the priority is getting into the field, earning trust with brand operators, and shipping two or three visible pilots before scaling through a team. This is a player-coach position built for someone who ships things.
[...]
• Own the company-wide AI enablement strategy: from identifying opportunities at the brand level to deploying tools, measuring adoption, and iterating.
[...]
• Evaluate SaaS and AI vendors — run proofs of concept, make build-vs-buy calls, and avoid tool sprawl.
[...]
• Identify capability gaps: where AI could work but isn't, and where current tools are underutilized.
[...]
You have hands-on depth with LLM-based tools — Anthropic Claude specifically. You can speak to what you've built, how you've deployed it, and what you've learned. You understand agentic workflows, multi-agent systems, and where AI creates real business leverage vs. noise.
[...]
• 7+ years in technology leadership, with at least 3 in AI, product, or digital transformation.
[...]
• Hands-on experience with LLM-based tools (Claude, OpenAI), workflow automation, and modern data stacks.
[...]
• Experience designing and deploying multi-agent AI systems — orchestrating agents, HITL checkpoints, drift monitoring, and incident runbooks.
[...]
A PE-backed, high-growth hospitality and property management platform operating 50+ brands across the US. The business has been under private equity ownership for approximately 10 years and is entering an aggressive growth phase — expected to double in size within 18 months through both organic growth and M&A. The company operates with a distributed, brand-level model across multiple US locations. Headcount in IT is approximately 10, with offshore resources and a dedicated data warehouse team already in place. The CEO reports directly to a PE sponsor with high expectations for technology-driven value creation.
[...]
The company is a heavy Claude user. They use a significant amount of off-the-shelf SaaS, are actively involved in M&A integration, and have begun building internal data and AI tools — but there’s no dedicated owner for AI enablement, reusable prompt libraries, capability gap identification, or field-level technology adoption. That gap is this job.
[...]
The Head of AI & Enablement will own the full AI lifecycle — strategy, tool evaluation, pilots, rollout, and measurement — across the entire brand portfolio. In year one, the priority is getting into the field, earning trust with brand operators, and shipping two or three visible pilots before scaling through a team. This is a player-coach position built for someone who ships things.
[...]
• Own the company-wide AI enablement strategy: from identifying opportunities at the brand level to deploying tools, measuring adoption, and iterating.
[...]

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote) | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote) | Wfh
[...]
# Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote)
[...]
Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity)
[...]
Department: Data Science
[...]
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
[...]
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
[...]
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
[...]
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
[...]
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
[...]
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
[...]
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
[...]
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
[...]
- Design intelligent sampling and evaluation strategies to measure rare events, policy recall, false positives/negatives and model blind spots in large-scale content systems
[...]
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
[...]
- Your Expertise:
[...]
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
[...]
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
[...]
- Working knowledge of causal inference
[...]
This position is US - Remote Eligible. The role may include occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager. While the position is Remote Eligible, you must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity. Click here for the up-to-date list of excluded states. This list is continuously evolving, so please check back with us if the state you live in is on the exclusion list. If your position is employed by another Airbnb entity, your recruiter will inform you what states you are eligible to work from.

results.title: Manager, Operations Performance and Data Analytics at Minor Hotels Europe and Americas - United States Remote
highlights:
Manager, Operations Performance and Data Analytics at Minor Hotels Europe and Americas - United States Remote
[...]
Minor Hotels Europe and Americas is a hospitality company that operates a diverse portfolio of hotels and resorts across Europe and the Americas. The company focuses on delivering exceptional guest experiences through high-quality service and unique accommodations, catering to both leisure and business travelers.
[...]
# Manager, Operations Performance and Data Analytics
[...]
Minor Hotels Europe and Americas is a hospitality company that operates a diverse portfolio of hotels and resorts across Europe and the Americas. The company focuses on delivering exceptional guest experiences through high-quality service and unique accommodations, catering to both leisure and business travelers.
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]

Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
[...]
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.

- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:

[...]

This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.

- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models

results.title: Shiv Misra Joins Marriott International to Lead Data, AI & Emerging Technologies - CDO Magazine
highlights:
# Shiv Misra Joins Marriott International to Lead Data, AI & Emerging Technologies
[...]
Shiv Misra, Marriott International Managing Vice President, Data, AI & Emerging Technologies
[...]
Marriott International has appointed Shiv Misra as Managing Vice President, Data, AI & Emerging Technologies, where he steps into a global technology leadership role overseeing the company’s enterprise data, analytics, AI, and emerging technology initiatives.
[...]
In this role, Misra will support enterprise modernization efforts and help advance next-generation technology capabilities across the global hospitality company’s customers, associates, and business operations. His responsibilities include driving innovation and operational impact through data platforms, AI solutions, and personalization capabilities, while also supporting emerging technology initiatives spanning areas such as Quantum Computing, Internet of Things (IoT), sensor-based technologies, and Digital Twins.
[...]
Prior to joining Marriott, Misra led enterprise AI, data, and technology transformation initiatives across Fortune 500 organizations spanning healthcare, hospitality, consumer goods, financial services, media, and technology. Most recently at McKesson, he architected the company’s enterprise AI strategy and modernization agenda from the ground up, establishing the operating models, governance, platforms, and scalable capabilities required to embed AI across the enterprise while helping unlock billions of dollars in incremental business impact.
[...]
Known for operating at the intersection of technology strategy and execution, Misra has built global AI and analytics organizations and led enterprise technology ecosystems designed to translate emerging technologies into measurable business outcomes.
[...]
Reflecting on the transition, Misra shared, “Returning to hospitality in a global technology leadership capacity feels deeply meaningful. I’m excited by the opportunity to help shape the future of data, AI, and emerging technologies at Marriott while building capabilities that strengthen innovation, elevate experiences, and create long-term enterprise value.” Shiv Misra holds a position on the CDO Magazine Global Board, showcasing his contributions to the field of data. He has been recognized on several industry lists, including Top 30 Healthcare Innovators 2026, 40 Under 40 Data Leaders 2025, and Data Leaders 100 2025.

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

results.title: #hospitalitytech #travelai #revenuemanagement #skiftsummit #leadership | Klaus Kohlmayr | 11 comments
highlights:
Yesterday I spent the day at the Skift AI and Data Summit in New York. A full room with some of the brightest minds in Travel. The common across many of the conversations: The technology is not the problem. We are. Booking.com, Expedia, IHG, TUI, Hilton — every company that shared real results today pointed to the same friction. Not the models, not the infrastructure. The organization. The culture. The deeply human difficulty of rewiring how decisions get made when the tools are suddenly moving faster than the people using them. As Vipul Hinge, interim CTO of Booking.com said: "The Human is fast becoming the bottleneck" And while most of us are still sorting that out, Skift Research already looked under the hood. They analyzed 170 AI job listings across 13 major public travel companies. The conclusion: most are renamers, not builders. Adding the word AI to a job description and calling it a strategy. Meanwhile, fewer than one in four travel companies have meaningfully scaled generative AI at all. The gap between the companies genuinely building and the ones performing AI is widening quickly. So what should senior leaders be doing now? 🛑 Stop calling things pilots. Commit, or don't. 💲 Make AI a business problem, not a technology project. The companies seeing real results have one thing in common — the person driving the AI agenda owns a revenue or cost line. No P&L attachment means no real accountability. 📈 Clean your data. Every speaker came back to this. The foundation determines everything above it. 🎯 Revisit what you reward people for. If one person with ten AI agents is doing the work of ten, how does your incentive reflect that? The window to be a fast follower is narrowing faster than most executive teams realize. Thanks Rafat and team for a great day full of insights, discovery and yes - real life conversations.#HospitalityTech#TravelAI#RevenueManagement#SkiftSummit#Leadership
[...]
Vice President of Hospitality Product | Vice President of Digital Transformation | United States | SaaS | Enterprise Software | Global Product & Portfolio | Customer-focused Innovation & Growth | AI Native
[...]
Great post Klaus Kohlmayr I fully agree. I see an average of 2-3 posts and announcements on LinkedIN claiming someone in Travel just introduced the "very first" AI solution; none are real, yet, none are bringing real value, yet, none are providing a real ROI as well as margin, yet... I am happy to be challenged on this, and happy for anyone to come challenge me at HITEC 😊 . The reality is, yes, we should leverage the solutions and technology, but with value in mind first, normalizing data, bringing something that changes their lives, makes them better, helps them make more revenue, and not just another post on LinkedIN.
[...]
The more we talk about AI, the clearer it becomes that the real challenge is rarely the tool itself. It is whether the organisation knows what problem it is trying to solve, who owns the outcome, and whether people are willing to change how decisions are made. In hospitality and travel, this is especially relevant because so much of the business sits across functions: commercial, operations, finance, marketing, distribution, guest experience, and technology. If AI stays in one corner of the business, it will not change much. The companies that make real progress will be the ones that connect AI to business priorities, clean data, decision discipline, and leadership accountability. Otherwise, it becomes another layer of activity without enough strategic movement behind it.
[...]
